episode.title: Six cars. One garage. Three seconds nobody can explain.

shots.caption: Every alarm on level B2 went off at 2:33 AM. For exactly three seconds.
shots.prompt: Level B2 sits still in the same fixed view: six parked cars in a row, concrete pillars, one distant fluorescent tube flickering slightly, the striped boom barrier down at the exit ramp. Nothing moves. Ends on: the exact same still garage.

In the same fixed view, the interior dome lights of ALL six cars blink on at the same instant; every hazard light flashes once in perfect sync; then all the cars go dark again together. Ends on: the dark, still row of cars.

In the same fixed view, a slow wave of dust and a few scraps of paper rolls across the concrete floor from the left toward the exit ramp, like a single pressure front passing through, and dies out. The cars do not move. Ends on: the floor settling, faint dust hanging in the air.

In the same fixed view, the striped boom barrier at the exit ramp rises smoothly on its own, holds fully open for a long moment over the empty ramp, then lowers itself back down. Nothing passes under it. Ends on: the barrier resting down, the ramp empty.

shots.caption: No car was missing. The ramp camera saw nothing leave.
shots.prompt: In the same fixed view, the garage is still — except the nearest car's door now stands slightly ajar with its dome light glowing softly inside the dark row. Ends on: the still garage, one door open, one small light on.
